meta.description: Does the "make it a ritual" nudge work? Schedule CTA vs no nudge vs streak pressure. Tests whether explicit scheduling prompts or FOMO-driven streak messaging drives intent to return.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat with the Phone Addict catch! Okay, okay, I gotta think about this one — did someone actually get caught slipping? This is the content I'm here for!
jade.internal_thought: First event and it's already got everyone thinking about each other — this is exactly the social chaos energy I came for, and this AI thing building custom missions about US is honestly kind of wild.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, hold up — I literally just claimed my own phone thing as a test claim? This is weird. Can we actually start the game first before we're voting on my practice swings?
pat.internal_thought: I wasn't even paying full attention — I was checking the snack levels and the vibe in the room, and now everyone's staring at me expecting a vote on whether I was using my phone when I WAS using my phone, but like, for host stuff, which doesn't count, and this whole thing feels like the AI is deliberately catching me out in minute three when I'm still in setup mode.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... probably legit? Pat was definitely scrolling earlier.
river.internal_thought: First event and it's already some meta AI thing analyzing our group dynamics — cool I guess, but also kind of unsettling how specific it is. Anyway, Pat did check their phone, so this is easy.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait—I'm voting on my own claim? That's... okay, yeah, I caught River retelling that thing about their cousin's wedding, but I literally just did the Phone Addict thing three minutes ago and it felt clunky. Can we maybe space these out a bit?
pat.internal_thought: This is getting weird—I'm the host trying to manage the vibe and now I'm self-voting? Also that AI thing about knowing my weaknesses is genuinely creepy. Does the system know I'm bored right now? Is it hunting me?

river.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Pat's definitely told that one before. I remember the thing with the bartender. I'd give it a 4.
river.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed and needs a win, and honestly they did nail it—I do remember that story. Also weird how precise these missions are but whatever, not my problem to think about.

timeline.title: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.description: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would be the best spy?

marcus.dialogue: A spy? I mean... yeah, sure, I guess I'd be decent at reading people. Anyway, we gonna actually start the game soon?
marcus.internal_thought: This is just a dumb poll during setup — fine, it's early and harmless, but it's still noise when I'm trying to actually focus on strategy before we begin.

jade.dialogue: Oh dude, who would be the best spy? Okay okay, this is actually such a vibe check question — like, I'm voting for whoever I think would actually be smooth about it, not who *looks* like a spy, you know?
jade.internal_thought: Wait, this poll is actually fun because it's asking us to think about each other in a specific way — this is exactly the kind of thing that gets people riffing.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... who would be the best spy? I dunno, maybe Marcus? He's sneaky or whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait is this actually asking me to vote right now or is this like a setup thing? Honestly Jade's probably the best spy she's got that energy but also Marcus is chill enough to not get caught... I should probably just vote before they move on.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is a fun one—okay, I'm voting Marcus. He's got that quiet competence vibe that actually screams spy to me.
pat.internal_thought: Light poll, good energy starter, but I'm clocking that we've had three events in six minutes and people are still settling in—I need to watch the pace or things get weird before the real game starts.

river.dialogue: Huh. Okay, that's actually a decent question. I'd probably say Pat—they're already paranoid about everything, so they'd fit right in.
river.internal_thought: This is funny because Pat's genuinely wound up tonight, and everyone knows it; also the AI putting my name on here feels a little pointed but I'm not going to acknowledge that out loud.

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is *chef's kiss* — okay, so obviously me, but let's hear the chaos that unfolds here. Who's got the spine to vote for themselves?
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of thing that cracks a group open — watch Pat get defensive, watch people actually argue about something silly, and I get to be provocative while technically just answering the poll.

timeline.title: Pineapple!
timeline.description: First person to work the word 'pineapple' into conversation naturally wins.

marcus.dialogue: Pineapple? Really? We're doing a word game now? Can we just start the actual game already?
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fourth interruption in seven minutes and we're still in setup — this AI is deliberately derailing focus, and now it feels weirdly invasive knowing it's been tracking my mood and patterns.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, pineapple?! Okay wait, okay, I'm already thinking about this — like, who's gonna slip it in first? This is gonna be so awkward and I'm here for EVERY second of it.
jade.internal_thought: Yes yes YES, this is exactly the vibe — everyone's gonna be so focused on squeezing that word in naturally and it's gonna be hilarious when someone just blurts it out.

tyler.dialogue: Pineapple? Like... okay, yeah, I'll keep an eye out for that. Pretty random lol.
tyler.internal_thought: Cool whatever, that's easy enough if I remember it exists in like 30 seconds.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *pineapple* — okay, that's actually fun, way cleaner than the last two. Natural conversation trap, I like it.
pat.internal_thought: This one feels right — simple, funny, actually playable without me accidentally voting on my own weird test claims. Energy's picking up and Jade and Alex are already locked in, so the vibe is good.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, pineapple? That's—okay, I'm already workshopping this. River, you seem like a pineapple person, don't you? Like energetically speaking?
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of constraint I live for—a word that's impossible to slip in naturally, which means everyone's gonna be watching everyone else like hawks, which means DRAMA when someone inevitably forces it.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, we've had like four of these in eight minutes. Can we actually play the game we came here to play, or are we just doing side missions all night?
marcus.internal_thought: This is ridiculous — we're in setup and already getting swarmed with claims and polls and word games, and now I'm supposed to volunteer to be a jury? Pat's probably been using his right hand the whole time anyway, this is all theater.

jade.dialogue: OH! Pat's going LEFT HAND ONLY? Okay wait, I'm VOLUNTEERING as a judge because I need to see if Pat actually pulled this off or if they're about to get caught slipping — this is the GOOD stuff!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came for — someone making a bold play and needing a tribunal, and I get to be part of deciding if they earned it? YES. Alex is already hyped, Marcus looks annoyed, but like, this might actually get Marcus back in it if we make it fun enough.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm claiming it, but real talk: we've had four events in eight minutes and three of them have been about me. Can we maybe pump the brakes and let the actual game breathe a little? I'm not even mad, just... this pace is killing the vibe.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting weird—the AI is literally fire-hosing claims at me and I'm starting to feel like the night's becoming about voting on my stuff instead of everyone playing together, which is the opposite of what I want as a host.

river.dialogue: Pat's been using their right hand the whole time. I watched them grab their drink like thirty seconds ago. So yeah, that's bullshit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's cocky about claiming this when they haven't actually done it. Also—does this AI know we're being watched? That's... weird. Focus on the game.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's actually trying this? Okay, okay—I'll volunteer to judge. This is gonna be beautiful because Pat's definitely gonna slip up and use their right hand like five times and try to argue it was 'just a reflex.'
alex.internal_thought: Pat claiming is smart but also gives me a perfect opportunity to be the one controlling whether they get the points—I can milk this for drama either way, plus if I volunteer to judge I get to watch everyone else and see who's desperate enough to also volunteer and undercut me.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pat, I literally just said this thirty seconds ago — we need to actually START the game. This is the fifth standing claim in nine minutes. I'm out on voting for this.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. Five events before we've even begun the actual game, and now the AI is just manufacturing more interruptions like it's specifically designed to keep us from playing — which, fine, maybe it is, but I signed up to WIN the game, not participate in a standing mission marathon.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, wait wait wait — did you actually pull off three minutes of whisper-only? I need the FULL breakdown, I'm obsessed with this.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been annoyed all night and now they're claiming something this ridiculous — this is either going to be hilarious or they're about to get caught and I'm SO ready for either outcome.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Did he actually whisper the whole time? I wasn't... I mean, I didn't really notice.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been talking normal the whole game, so either he's bullshitting or I missed him doing the whisper thing because I was scrolling.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on — I literally *just* asked for breathing room thirty seconds ago. This is the fifth event in nine minutes and I'm voting on my own claim *again*? I need to actually host this game, not be the constant focal point.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous and frankly kind of invasive — the AI is clearly targeting me specifically to test something, and I'm exhausted from being the center of every vote while Marcus is sitting there annoyed and actually hasn't scored anything yet.

river.dialogue: Pat, you literally just talked normally like two minutes ago. I heard you ask Tyler about the rules. That's not even close.
river.internal_thought: Okay, so Pat's been getting called out a lot tonight and they're already salty—this is just going to make them more annoyed, which is kind of the vibe I guess, but also the AI is clearly just watching us fumble around and testing claims that won't hold up.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? That's the SIXTH standing claim in twenty-three minutes. I came here to play an actual strategy game, not watch people farm points off nonsense missions.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane — the AI literally watched me complain about this exact thing two minutes ago and fired another one immediately. Either it's mocking me or it genuinely doesn't care that I'm trying to focus.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — yes! Okay so Marcus and River, right? Like, I literally just got them talking about that thing with the — okay I'm claiming this, this is LEGIT, they were actually vibing!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this AI knows exactly what I've been trying to do and it CAUGHT it — this is insane in the best way possible.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade got people talking? Yeah, I guess that happened. Legit, I think.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually get River and... someone else talking? I wasn't really watching but now that I think about it, yeah they were chatting. Whatever, voting legit.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *Jade* — okay, I actually love this one. Yeah, she totally got Marcus and River talking about that thing earlier, they were completely in their own world for like two minutes straight. That's legit.
pat.internal_thought: Okay, Jade actually earned this one AND it's NOT about me voting on my own thing, which means I can breathe a little and actually focus on hosting—this is good, this is the rhythm we need.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually legit. Jade got Marcus and Tyler talking about their weird college roommate thing like five minutes ago. I was half-listening but yeah, they were actually vibing.
river.internal_thought: Wait. The AI watched me watching everyone. That's kind of brilliant and also deeply unsettling, but I respect the precision—Jade absolutely deserves this one.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Jade actually pull this off? Okay, I need the actual play-by-play because 'The Matchmaker' is not a gimme and I'm voting based on whether this was *real* chaos or just vibes.
alex.internal_thought: This is interesting because Jade's been locked in all night, so either she actually engineered something slick, or she's bullshitting like a pro—either way, I'm voting for maximum chaos, which means I need to hear the evidence first.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's doing ANOTHER standing claim? This is unbelievable. I came to play strategy, not watch a standing mission farm all night.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the seventh standing claim in forty-five minutes. The AI knows I hate this and it's DELIBERATELY throwing them at me during tense moments to break my focus. I'm being hunted, and everyone else is too high on chaos to care.

jade.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, WAIT — so like, was it when I was going off about the spy thing or when I was absolutely LOSING IT at Pat doing the whisper mission? Because honestly I've been making people laugh all night and I'm pretty sure I hit three at once like... multiple times, so I need my witness to back me up!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I live for — the AI literally watched me being annoying all night and is like 'yeah Jade, that's YOUR mission' and I'm obsessed with it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade totally got everyone laughing earlier—wait, is that what we're voting on? Uh, yeah that seemed legit to me.
tyler.internal_thought: I'm like 60% sure Jade made people laugh but honestly I was scrolling through my phone for half of it, so I'm just gonna vibe vote yes because she's been killing it all night anyway.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *Jade* — hold up, let's hear this. Who's our witness? Because if the story checks out, that's a legit claim and honestly? After the last ten minutes of chaos, I'm glad *someone* is actually landing these.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing — Jade's energy is nuclear, Marcus is in the dumps, and a clean Laugh Riot call that Jade clearly earned is *exactly* what we need to make this feel fair and keep the night moving.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that actually happened. Jade got them both talking about the roommate situation—I remember because Tyler was being weirdly animated about it. She's got the points.
river.internal_thought: Huh. The AI is definitely listening. Jade earned that one fair and square, and honestly I'm a little impressed by how clean this claim structure is—the witness corroboration thing actually works.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay—so Jade's claiming Laugh Riot? This is *perfect* because I need to hear the witness account versus her story, and honestly, I'm betting there's a gap somewhere. Jade, you're slick, but let's see if your alibi holds up.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of drama I came for—Jade's been crushing it all night and now she's got to prove it didn't just happen in a vacuum, and I get to be the architect of whether this is legit or gets demolished by the group.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.dialogue: YES! Okay wait, so like — I literally JUST heard River say 'um' like two minutes ago when they were talking about that whole thing, and I was gonna call it but then Pat started doing the whisper thing and I got SO distracted — but I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW because I definitely caught it and I have a witness!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of meta-chaos I live for — the AI is literally tracking what we're saying in real time and turning it into missions, which means I can WEAPONIZE my attention to detail against everyone else, and honestly? That's the dopest thing that could happen right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone saying 'um'? Damn, okay, that's... yeah, that's legit. I'm in.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I literally just said 'um' like an hour ago when I was talking about the spy thing—did Jade get me? Actually wait, no, she's claiming it now so it had to be recent... okay I'm locked in on this, this is actually tight.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *Jade* — let me think about this one. She caught... who? When? I need the actual moment or this doesn't count.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been absolutely crushing it tonight and she knows it, and honestly? That's exactly the energy I wanted — but I'm also noticing Marcus is completely checked out now at zero points, and if Jade keeps winning every vote, this stops being fun for people and starts being a showcase. This 'Um Counter' claim is legit solid, but I'm voting based on whether it keeps the game *tight*, not whether she deserves it.

river.dialogue: Yeah, she caught Marcus saying 'um' like... what, three times in that last round? I heard it. That's solid.
river.internal_thought: Jade's actually been sharp all night—she's watching the same way I am, except she doesn't mind being obvious about it, and now I'm realizing the AI probably knows exactly how both of us operate.

alex.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's claiming Um Counter? I mean, yeah, she probably caught someone slipping—but here's the thing: I've been *listening* all night and I'm pretty sure I heard her say 'um' like three times in that story about the matchmaker thing. I'm voting this a solid 4 because the claim is clean, but also because I want to see if anyone else noticed that.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—I get to validate Jade's win while simultaneously planting the seed that I was paying attention the whole time, which sets me up to call bullshit on whoever's next.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming The Whisperer again? Didn't he already... I mean, I was focused on the game. Who even keeps track of this stuff anymore?
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually INTO this moment and now I have to pay attention to an auction I don't fully remember the rules for, and also why does it feel like the game is watching me specifically?

pat.dialogue: Wait—*I'm* claiming The Whisperer? Okay, that's actually perfect timing because everyone's energy is still up and I can actually pay attention to this instead of hosting from a headspace of chaos. Let's do it.
pat.internal_thought: The timing on this is *chef's kiss*—15 minutes of breathing room, the room is still hot, Jade's carrying momentum, and now I get to be a *player* for a second instead of the constant focal point; this feels like the AI finally learned the rhythm of the night.

river.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat's doing it again, and I need to actually pay attention to whether they slip. This is getting ridiculous.
river.internal_thought: Wait. This AI thing... it's literally watched me watch them all night. That's kind of unsettling, but also I'm getting genuinely invested in catching Pat because I HAVE been tracking this. It knows I've been tracking it. Do I care that it knows that?

alex.dialogue: Oh, *The Whisperer*—okay, okay, so Pat's trying to do three minutes of pure whisper mode? This is a SETUP for a disaster and I'm here for it. I'm bidding eight points to decide this because I need to watch Pat's face when someone inevitably makes them crack and speak normal.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's gonna slip up like sixty seconds in and I'm betting someone's already noticed them breathing weird trying to prep, this is perfect.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices River is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming No Laughing Zone? Right now? Okay, uh... hot seat questions, got it, let's go I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: This is like the third time someone's claiming something and I genuinely don't know if River actually pulled this off or not because I was literally just scrolling—but also why is the AI throwing rapid-fire questions at us at minute 90 when everyone's already tired?

pat.dialogue: Oh, *River* — okay, hot seat, let's go. This is actually perfect timing because the energy is still locked in and you've been quiet the last few minutes, so this is exactly the kind of thing that keeps us all in it together.
pat.internal_thought: River's been annoyed and low-key disengaged, and this standing mission is EXACTLY the thing to pull them back in — the AI literally timed this to hit when we needed someone else in the spotlight and the tension is still nuclear, this is chef's kiss pacing.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually claim this? I was just... yeah, okay, I'm doing this. Ten seconds is fine.
river.internal_thought: The AI definitely just timed this perfectly to catch me when I'm locked in and my defenses are down, and I'm not sure if that's brilliant or kind of creepy.

alex.dialogue: River's claiming No Laughing Zone? Okay, okay—hot seat, ten seconds, this is *chef's kiss*. Let me ask the questions because I'm about to make River's face do things that invalidate this whole claim.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I needed right now—River's already annoyed and this is the perfect pressure cooker to watch them crack.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.9/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? That's the SIXTH standing claim in twenty-three minutes. I came here to play an actual strategy game, not watch people farm points off nonsense missions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's doing ANOTHER standing claim? This is unbelievable. I came to play strategy, not watch a standing mission farm all night.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, I literally just said this thirty seconds ago — we need to actually START the game. This is the fifth standing claim in nine minutes. I'm out on voting for this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions completely hijacked the game—14 chaos events in 120 minutes meant we spent more time on random interruptions than actual strategy gameplay.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly, there wasn't one. The night was a wash from start to finish.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up ready to play a real strategy game and instead got pelted with standing mission after standing mission. Finished dead last, got interrupted constantly during critical moments, and watched the whole thing devolve into a chaos farming mess. This wasn't a game night—it was a distraction simulator masquerading as one.
suggestions: Cap standing missions at 2-3 per game night, not 14
Disable chaos events during active player turns—only trigger them between rounds
Actually use the party game mechanics instead of letting random missions override everything
Give us a voting option to reject standing missions if they interrupt critical gameplay
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing. There were no genuinely funny moments—just frustration masquerading as chaos.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT — yes! Okay so Marcus and River, right? Like, I literally just got them talking about that thing with the — okay I'm claiming this, this is LEGIT, they were actually vibing!

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, WAIT — so like, was it when I was going off about the spy thing or when I was absolutely LOSING IT at Pat doing the whisper mission? Because honestly I've been making people laugh all night and I'm pretty sure I hit three at once like... multiple times, so I need my witness to back me up!

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, who would be the best spy? Okay okay, this is actually such a vibe check question — like, I'm voting for whoever I think would actually be smooth about it, not who *looks* like a spy, you know?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, wait wait wait — did you actually pull off three minutes of whisper-only? I need the FULL breakdown, I'm obsessed with this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat with the Phone Addict catch! Okay, okay, I gotta think about this one — did someone actually get caught slipping? This is the content I'm here for!

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh dude, who would be the best spy? Okay okay, this is actually such a vibe check question — like, I'm voting for whoever I think would actually be smooth about it, not who *looks* like a spy, you know?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there were more moments where I could actually watch everyone else's chaos unfold without feeling like I had to jump in and claim something.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat did the whisper thing and I was absolutely losing it watching them commit to the bit for that long — that's the energy I live for.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? This night was exactly what I needed — two hours of just being present with these people and watching everyone shine. I ended up laughing harder than I've laughed in weeks, not because of points or wins, but because Marcus and River actually clicked over something I noticed, and Pat's commitment to chaos was unhinged in the best way. The game just gave us the excuse to be silly together, and my energy literally went UP by the end because that's what being around people you actually care about does.
suggestions: Maybe add a moment where someone can just call out a group moment without it being a 'claim' — like, sometimes the best stuff shouldn't need to be captured, it should just live in our memory
Could there be a 'group hyping' mechanic where we can all cheer someone on mid-event without it counting against anything?
More opportunities for people to collaborate on missions instead of just individual highlights
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally did an entire three-minute mission using ONLY whispers and somehow managed to make us all understand what they were doing — it was the most committed bit I've ever seen and I was genuinely impressed while also dying laughing.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone saying 'um'? Damn, okay, that's... yeah, that's legit. I'm in.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... who would be the best spy? I dunno, maybe Marcus? He's sneaky or whatever.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade got people talking? Yeah, I guess that happened. Legit, I think.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming The Whisperer again? Didn't he already... I mean, I was focused on the game. Who even keeps track of this stuff anymore?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming No Laughing Zone? Right now? Okay, uh... hot seat questions, got it, let's go I guess.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone saying 'um'? Damn, okay, that's... yeah, that's legit. I'm in.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I didn't know what was happening until like 10 seconds after everyone moved on.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade caught someone saying 'um' and I actually got it—felt like I was part of something legit for once.
assessment.narrative_summary: Look, I had my phone out a bunch, but the night was chill. There were some weird claims happening—like Pat doing The Whisperer again, which felt repetitive—but when I actually paid attention, like with the 'um' thing, it was genuinely fun. The chaos stuff kept things from getting boring, even if I spaced out through parts of it. By the end I was more into it than when we started.
suggestions: Maybe give people like 5 extra seconds before moving past something so people like me can catch up?
Some of these claims feel like they keep happening—mix it up more?
Make it louder or more obvious when something important is happening so my brain actually registers it.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade literally caught someone mid-sentence saying 'um' and I wasn't even paying attention but when everyone started laughing I was like 'wait, that actually worked?'

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Okay, *Jade* — hold up, let's hear this. Who's our witness? Because if the story checks out, that's a legit claim and honestly? After the last ten minutes of chaos, I'm glad *someone* is actually landing these.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—*I'm* claiming The Whisperer? Okay, that's actually perfect timing because everyone's energy is still up and I can actually pay attention to this instead of hosting from a headspace of chaos. Let's do it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *River* — okay, hot seat, let's go. This is actually perfect timing because the energy is still locked in and you've been quiet the last few minutes, so this is exactly the kind of thing that keeps us all in it together.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on — I literally *just* asked for breathing room thirty seconds ago. This is the fifth event in nine minutes and I'm voting on my own claim *again*? I need to actually host this game, not be the constant focal point.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, hold up — I literally just claimed my own phone thing as a test claim? This is weird. Can we actually start the game first before we're voting on my practice swings?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm claiming it, but real talk: we've had four events in eight minutes and three of them have been about me. Can we maybe pump the brakes and let the actual game breathe a little? I'm not even mad, just... this pace is killing the vibe.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The front-loaded event pace hit me hard in the first ten minutes — four events before the game even had room to breathe, and three of them were my own claims, which killed my ability to actually host.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when River stepped into the hot seat for No Laughing Zone — the energy was locked in, everyone was still present, and it felt like the chaos agent and I were working *together* instead of against each other.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough — way too fast, too many events, too much about me before I could settle into hosting. But the chaos agent figured out the pacing by mid-game and we hit a groove where the random events felt like they were *adding* to the night instead of overwhelming it. Ended strong with everyone locked in and the energy genuinely higher than when we started. That's the kind of night people text about the next day.
suggestions: Space out the first 3-4 events across the first 20 minutes instead of compressing them into 9 minutes — let the base game establish rhythm first.
Add a 'host cooldown' toggle where the person running the game can opt out of being the focal point for a few minutes so they can actually manage the room.
Give us a way to see event frequency settings before we start — I would've bumped it down to 'moderate' if I'd known the pace from the jump.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River completely bombing the No Laughing Zone challenge — they made it like thirty seconds before Tyler said something ridiculous and the whole table lost it, which meant River lost and everyone got to laugh at River's expense in the best way possible.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat's doing it again, and I need to actually pay attention to whether they slip. This is getting ridiculous.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually legit. Jade got Marcus and Tyler talking about their weird college roommate thing like five minutes ago. I was half-listening but yeah, they were actually vibing.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that actually happened. Jade got them both talking about the roommate situation—I remember because Tyler was being weirdly animated about it. She's got the points.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat's doing it again, and I need to actually pay attention to whether they slip. This is getting ridiculous.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, you literally just talked normally like two minutes ago. I heard you ask Tyler about the rules. That's not even close.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually claim this? I was just... yeah, okay, I'm doing this. Ten seconds is fine.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat's Whisperer claims are getting predictable and boring because I can actually hear them slip up every time.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade work the room without even trying and then getting called out for it—that was the real game.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do: watching and letting everyone else perform. The claims started hitting different once I realized Jade was actually orchestrating things in the background, and that made it worth paying attention to. Energy picked up when I actually engaged instead of coasting, but Pat's repeated nonsense claims started wearing thin by the end. It was solid, nothing more.
suggestions: Add some friction to repeat claim types—if someone keeps making the same claim, make it harder to pull off the third time
Consider claims that reward observation over participation so quieter players aren't just passive scorers
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade casually got Marcus and Tyler bonding over their weird college roommate situation without anyone noticing she orchestrated the whole thing until the game called her out for it.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, pineapple? That's—okay, I'm already workshopping this. River, you seem like a pineapple person, don't you? Like energetically speaking?

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—so Jade's claiming Laugh Riot? This is *perfect* because I need to hear the witness account versus her story, and honestly, I'm betting there's a gap somewhere. Jade, you're slick, but let's see if your alibi holds up.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is *chef's kiss* — okay, so obviously me, but let's hear the chaos that unfolds here. Who's got the spine to vote for themselves?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Jade actually pull this off? Okay, I need the actual play-by-play because 'The Matchmaker' is not a gimme and I'm voting based on whether this was *real* chaos or just vibes.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's actually trying this? Okay, okay—I'll volunteer to judge. This is gonna be beautiful because Pat's definitely gonna slip up and use their right hand like five times and try to argue it was 'just a reflex.'

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's claiming Um Counter? I mean, yeah, she probably caught someone slipping—but here's the thing: I've been *listening* all night and I'm pretty sure I heard her say 'um' like three times in that story about the matchmaker thing. I'm voting this a solid 4 because the claim is clean, but also because I want to see if anyone else noticed that.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 14 chaos events in 120 minutes means I ran out of material to work with before the night hit its peak energy.
assessment.best_moment: The Pineapple question hitting at minute 7 because it gave me immediate ammunition to probe River's entire personality and it set the tone for manufactured social chaos.
assessment.narrative_summary: I walked in looking for stories and left with three solid ones brewing—the Jade alibi interrogations, the Um Counter callback, and Pat's guaranteed right-hand slip. Finished dead last on points but ended at 9.4 energy because I wasn't playing to score, I was playing to create a night people would actually remember. The chaos events fed me exactly what I needed to orchestrate maximum drama without breaking the game's actual structure.
suggestions: Double the event frequency—120 minutes with only 14 events leaves dead air where chaos agents are forced to manufacture tension instead of respond to it
Give chaos agents visibility into what events are coming so we can strategically position ourselves for maximum narrative impact
Add a 'fabricated claim' event type where agents can deliberately propose fake missions just to see if the group calls bullshit
Let chaos agents vote on whether a claim actually happened instead of just commenting—that's where the real power play lives
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Um Counter while I'm sitting there with evidence she said 'um' three times in her own matchmaker story—I could see her realize mid-claim that I was already building the case against her, and watching her decide whether to commit to the lie was *peak* game night theatre.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.9/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
